title: ChoiceProperty Object
description: A property that is a predefined list of choices. This is most commonly used for properties associated with materials and appearances.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: getChoices
methods.description: Method that returns the list of available choices.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the unique ID of this property.

properties.name: isReadOnly
properties.description: Indicates if this property is read-only. If True any attempted edits will fail.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of this property as seen in the user interface. This name is localized and can change based on the current language

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parent
properties.description: Returns the parent of this property.For properties associated with an appearance this will return the parent Appearance. For properties associated with a material, this will return the parent Material. For other types of properties, this will return the PropertyGroup it is in.

properties.name: value
properties.description: Gets and sets the which choice is selected from the set of choices. The value is a string that matches one of the predefined choices. The names of the available choices can be obtained using GetChoices method.

classType.title: ChoiceProperty.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

getChoices.title: ChoiceProperty.getChoices Method
getChoices.description: Method that returns the list of available choices.
getChoices.return_description: Returns true if the call was successful.

parameters.name: names
parameters.description: An array of the names of the choices. These coincide with the array of choices returned by the choices argument.

parameters.name: choices
parameters.description: An array of the choices. These coincide with the array of names returned by the names argument.

id.title: ChoiceProperty.id Property
id.description: Returns the unique ID of this property.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = choiceProperty_var.id

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

parent.title: ChoiceProperty.parent Property
parent.description: Returns the parent of this property.For properties associated with an appearance this will return the parent Appearance. For properties associated with a material, this will return the parent Material. For other types of properties, this will return the PropertyGroup it is in.

value.title: ChoiceProperty.value Property
value.description: Gets and sets the which choice is selected from the set of choices. The value is a string that matches one of the predefined choices. The names of the available choices can be obtained using GetChoices method.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = choiceProperty_var.value

# Set the value of the property.
choiceProperty_var.value = propertyValue
